The highest level, you know, sales is the transference of emotion. And the primary emotion that you're transferring is certainty, right? There's certainty about yourself, certainty about your product, certainty about the company you work for, those three tens, right? Both on a logical and emotional level. And essentially what you're doing is you are transferring that white hot certainty that you feel inside you. You're transferring it to the person that you're speaking to, the prospect who is not at a nearly higher level of certainty. In other words, you walk into a sales counter. Just watch what's happening. Just never forget this. When you enter a sales encounter, right, you, the salesperson, you know all the features and benefits of your product. You know how awesome it is, you know how amazing the value proposition is, right? You're aware of all that. So because of that, you know how awesome it is and you're excited about it. And that excitement essentially comes through your voice, your demeanor and your by-lounge and through the words that you say, right? And if you're not excited, if that doesn't come through, well, good look. Trying to transfer that emotion that you feel like to someone else. That's what's going on. You're transferring that level of enthusiasm, excitement, rolled up into that one word, certainty, right? So that's what's going on. Now the straight line system, what it represents essentially is the methodology by which you transfer certainty. In other words, it allows you to take the certainty that you're feeling inside of yourself. So like assuming you are really, really certain, it shows you how to transfer that certainty to another human being in a way that doesn't violate the overarching rules of success. Meaning that you can't break for poor, that the whole energy in benefits out equation, all these different things that I mentioned at also this week. But here's the point I want to raise here. I haven't spoken about in a while. That is the state that you're in, the physiological, emotional state and your ability to manage that state in the key moments in your life when it matters most, not all the time. So again, you might have heard me say this before, so you know, what do you call someone who walks around all day long with their chest puffed out and their shoulders out and you know, all over enthusiastic, they walk around in that state of absolute positive and certainty. Certainty, what do you call that person? Call them an, right? We hate those people. We hate those people who are always so certain about everything. So it's not what state management is all about. Remember, state management is about those key moments in life that you need to trigger a peak state, those moments where it matters most. Like when you walk in the door to open up a cell, when you pick up the phone, someone walks in your office, when you're negotiating a deal. But in those key moments, that's when you want to be able to trigger the state of absolute certainty. Because what happens is, is the certainty that you then you trigger that state. So you're in a state of certainty and it comes through because how do you express certainty? Does all human beings express their emotions in generally the same way? In other words, someone from Papua New Guinea or Australia or freaking the North Pole, it doesn't matter where they're born, where they grew up, when they are certain about something, they act the same way.
